steps.title: Use the y-intercept to find the constant term
steps.content: When $x = 0$, the quadratic $y = ax^2 + bx + c$ reduces to $y = c$. Since $y = 45$ when $x = 0$, the constant term is $c = 45$. This narrows the choices to A, B, and C.

steps.title: Backsolve choice B
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B ($y = -x^2 + 12x + 45$):

At $x = 6$: $y = -(6)^2 + 12(6) + 45 = -36 + 72 + 45 = 81$ ✓

At $x = 15$: $y = -(15)^2 + 12(15) + 45 = -225 + 180 + 45 = 0$ ✓

All three given points satisfy this equation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
